diagrams.title: Span of Single Vector in R²
diagrams.description: Shows how a single non-zero vector creates a line through origin when multiplied by all scalars
elements: Line through origin in direction of v
Arrow showing line extends infinitely in both directions
diagrams.purpose: Visualize that span of one vector is a 1-dimensional subspace (a line)

diagrams.title: Span of Two Vectors in R²
diagrams.description: Two linearly independent vectors create the entire R² plane through linear combinations
diagrams.purpose: Understand that 2 independent vectors span the entire 2D space

diagrams.title: Span of Dependent Vectors
diagrams.description: Two linearly dependent vectors (one is multiple of other) span only a line, not the full plane
diagrams.purpose: Show that dependence reduces span - adding dependent vectors doesn't expand span

diagrams.title: Span in R³ - Progression
diagrams.description: Shows how 1, 2, and 3 vectors create line, plane, and full space respectively
elements: Plane through origin (span of 2 independent vectors)
diagrams.purpose: Extend span visualization to 3D, showing dimension increases with independent vectors

diagrams.title: Span as Subspace
diagrams.description: Illustration showing that span always contains origin and is closed under operations
elements: Sum of two vectors in span
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof that span is always a subspace
